entitySuffixRule: Must contain the words "limited liability company" or the abbreviation "L.L.C." or "LLC" (or a similar abbreviation). The name may include the name of a member or manager.
distinguishabilityNote: Must be distinguishable upon the records of the Secretary of State from the name of any entity incorporated, authorized, formed, or registered in New Hampshire under RSA 292, 293-A, 293-B, 294-A, 301, 301-A, 304-A, 304-B, 304-C, 305-A, or the trade name chapter RSA 349. New Hampshire spells out differences that do not make a name distinguishable, including a prefix, suffix, or other derivative of the same word, a change in an entity-status word, an added numeral (absent the current holder's consent), and punctuation or special-character differences that do not change the meaning. A non-distinguishable name may be used with written consent, or by adding words when the name is identical.
nameReservationCostBasis: The Secretary of State fee for an Application for Reservation of Name is $15.
notes: New Hampshire's LLC Act (RSA 304-C) sets a strict distinguishability test. Section 304-C:32 folds registered trade names (RSA 349) into the entity-name screen and lists what does not count as a difference, so a prefix, a plural, an added number, or a punctuation change will not clear a conflicting name. There is no banned-word list in the section. A reservation lasts 120 days. New Hampshire's LLC formation fee and annual report are each $102 (a $100 fee plus a $2 online surcharge), and the annual report is due April 1.
